nodes.title: Require a plain object input
nodes.why: This keeps the helper strict and predictable by rejecting invalid shapes early.
nodes.alternative: Silently return an empty string for invalid inputs.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter API, but easier to debug incorrect calls.

nodes.title: Sort keys for stable query order
nodes.why: Stable ordering makes output deterministic, which helps tests and caching.
nodes.tradeoff: Small sort cost, but consistent output every time.

nodes.title: Skip undefined values completely
nodes.why: Undefined usually means “do not include this key” in query strings.
nodes.alternative: Serialize undefined as an empty value.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner URLs, but undefined cannot be represented explicitly.

nodes.title: Convert null into an empty value
nodes.why: This supports the rule that null should serialize as key=.
nodes.alternative: Turn null into the literal string "null".
nodes.tradeoff: Matches common URL behavior, but loses distinction between null and empty string.

nodes.title: Serialize arrays as repeated key pairs
nodes.why: Repeating the same key is a widely used format for array query params.
nodes.alternative: Serialize arrays as a comma-separated single value.
nodes.tradeoff: Longer query strings, but better compatibility with common parsers.

nodes.title: Build pairs first, then join with &
nodes.why: Collecting pairs avoids tricky leading/trailing & logic and keeps code easy to follow.
nodes.alternative: Append directly to one string while looping.
nodes.tradeoff: Uses a temporary array, but simpler and less error-prone.
